contribute.title: Submit images or a new POI
contribute.body: Send images via Discord or open a pull request on GitHub. New POIs live as markdown under content/community-poi/.

collaboration.open_body: This project welcomes images, materials, and building hands. Coordinate on the forum channel or Discord before you start.
collaboration.closed_body: This project is not maintained by the community. Admire it freely, but please don't build or change anything — use the forum channel for feedback and questions.

litematica.title: Set up & use Litematica
litematica.intro: Quick-start and FAQ around the Litematica mod so you can use the schematics from this overview right away.

rule.title: Server rule — important!
rule.body: On OneLiteFeather, Litematica is only allowed as a blueprint (hologram view). Auto-place (Easy Place / Place Holder / any automated block-setting) is forbidden and will be punished with a permanent ban. Place the blocks yourself — the hologram is a guide, not a building tool.

entries.a: Litematica is a Minecraft client mod (Fabric) that displays schematic files (.litematic) as a hologram in-game. You can rebuild any project 1:1 without touching the server — other players don't see your hologram.

entries.q: How do I install the mod?
entries.a: You need the Fabric loader for your Minecraft version and drop the Litematica JAR (plus its dependency MaLiLib) into the mods/ folder. After launch, check the mods menu to confirm both are active.

entries.q: How do I load a schematic?
entries.a: Save the .litematic file in .minecraft/schematics/. In-game, open the Litematica menu (default: M), pick 'Load schematic' and set the position via 'Schematic placement'.

entries.q: What's the point / setup guide for?
entries.a: Each schematic ships with a recommended origin and facing direction. Following them places the hologram in the intended spot and aligns it with neighbouring builds.

entries.q: Which Litematica version do I need?
entries.a: Each schematic lists the recommended Litematica version (and matching Minecraft version) right next to the file. Stick to that combination — older or newer Litematica releases can serialise blocks differently, which shifts or breaks the hologram.

entries.q: Can I use auto-place / easy place?
entries.a: No. OneLiteFeather forbids any kind of auto-place — that includes Easy Place, Place Holder and similar features that set blocks automatically. Violations lead to a permanent ban. Litematica is only allowed as a visual blueprint; you place every block yourself.

entries.q: Can I use the schematics on my own world?
entries.a: Yes, of course. The schematics in this overview are free to use privately and on your own servers. Please credit the original builders when sharing publicly.
